---
title: "UIAO Education — The Comics Tell the Story"
date: "2026-04-17"
author: "Michal Doroszewski"
description: "Narrative-led onboarding for anyone new to UIAO. Training teaches how to use and contribute; Education builds the mental model that makes the training stick."
categories: [education, narrative, onboarding, comics, governance]
aspirational: true
---

# UIAO Education — The Comics Tell the Story

Before you can use UIAO, you have to see what it is. That is what
the Education program does. It is distinct from [Training
(UIAO_125)](program-training.qmd): **Training teaches how to use
and contribute to the substrate; Education builds the conceptual
mental model that makes the training stick.**

The canonical syllabus is [UIAO_128 — Education
Program](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/core/canon/specs/program-education.md).

## The comic-led modules

The program is led by a series of illustrated comics — one image
per core abstraction, each rendered so that a CIO, CISO,
compliance lead, or new agency staffer can absorb the concept in
the time it takes to look at a picture.

| Module | Concept |
|---|---|
| The Governance OS | UIAO is an *operating system for governance*, not a scanner or a doc generator |
| The Canonical Object | How an object gets one canonical record — the SSOT anchor |
| The Unified Environment Map | How adapters collapse many vendor surfaces into one map |
| Provenance | Why every artifact traces back to a canonical source |
| The Modernization Lifecycle | How a modernization runs end-to-end through UIAO |
| Address Governance | The directory / IPAM flow formerly in GOS |
| Drift Detection | The closed loop: scan → classify → remediate |
| Drift Remediation | How a finding becomes a POA&M item and then a closed ticket |

The comic assets live at
`docs/publications/series-assets/UIAO-*.jpg`. Each one pairs with
a short written walkthrough that grounds it in the canon and
closes with one concrete question the reader should now be able to
answer.

## The learning arc

The program is not a feature tour. It builds a mental model in a
specific order.

1. Governance is a **system**, not a report or a checklist.
2. **Identity** is the primitive. Everything else attaches to a
   canonical object.
3. **Maps** replace inventories. Adapters produce one unified
   environment map, not a pile of spreadsheets.
4. **Provenance** is non-negotiable. Every derived artifact names
   its canonical source and version.
5. **Modernization** is a lifecycle, not a project. UIAO runs it
   end-to-end.
6. **Drift** is the feedback signal. The substrate knows when
   reality has diverged from canon and reports it in a
   classified, prioritized form.

A learner who internalizes those six ideas is ready to enter
Training and pick the Operator or Contributor track.

## Formats

- **90-minute facilitated walkthrough** for agency kickoff. One
  comic per ~10 minutes, Q&A between.
- **Self-serve written path** for anyone who cannot attend live.
  Sufficient on its own.
- **45-minute auditor briefing** — Provenance, Drift Detection,
  and Drift Remediation only.

## Companion pages

- [Training](program-training.qmd) — the how, once the why is in place
- [Test Plans](program-test-plans.qmd) — the catalog of what tests govern what
- [Project Plans](program-project-plans.qmd) — the roadmap and agency templates
